perturbation.original_text: The Inform ation of each party is and shall rem ain that party’s sole, exclusive and valuable property. Nothing in this Ag reem ent or in any disclosures o f Information by either party sh all be construed as granting or conferrin g to the other party an y licen se, rig hts, title o r any interest wh atsoever in any such Inform ation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Inform ation of each party is and shall rem ain that party’s sole, exclusive and valuable property. Nothing in this Ag reem ent or in any disclosures o f Information by either party sh all be construed as granting or conferrin g to the other party an y license.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'rights, title or any interest whatsoever in any such Information' after 'license' weakens the protection of proprietary rights. While a license is explicitly not granted, the omission suggests that other implicit rights *might* be conferred, leading to potential disputes over ownership and usage. This contradicts the initial statement that the information remains the 'sole, exclusive and valuable property' of the disclosing party. This is an in-text contradiction because, in this section, the agreement grants no rights, yet implies they could still exist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Inform ation of each party is and shall rem ain that party’s sole, exclusive and valuable property. Nothing in this Ag reem ent or in any disclosures o f Information by either party sh all be construed as granting or conferrin g to the other party an y license.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies the granting of any 'rights, title or any interest whatsoever' beyond a license. The changed text removes this explicit denial, implying that rights, title, or interest *could* be granted implicitly, which contradicts the statement found in the Contradicted Text: 'The Information of each party is and shall remain that party’s sole, exclusive and valuable property' - thereby creating a direct conflict and thus, an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each part y shall hold in trust and confi dence and prot ect and treat as strictly secret and confidential at all times the Inform ation received from the other party, and in respect of su ch Inform ation, each party agrees that it: (a) shall not directly o r indirectly d isclose, verbally, in writin g or in any other form, or allow the Inform ation to be di sclosed to any other person, fi rm or com pany; (b) shall keep secret and shall not use, m anipulate or exploit the Information, except for the Purpose and then only in strict accordan ce with the Purpose and the terms of this Agreem ent; (c) shall restrict disclosure of th e Inform ation to such of i ts employees or consul tants or subcont ractors who need t o know for t he Purpose and shal l procure t hat such persons observe ful ly the provi sions of this Ag reem ent as if th ey were th emselves parties to it; (d) shall not copy , store or di stribute any Inform ation or al low any Inform ation to be copi ed, st ored or distributed except for such copi es, st orage or di stribution as are st rictly requi red for t he Purpose; NDA Page 2 of 2 (e) shall protect the com mercial inte rest of the disclosing party and sh all take the sam e precautions to protect the Inform ation as a careful and prudent person woul d be expect ed to employ for i ts own business and confi dential inform ation and, i n doing so, shal l use reasonabl e care t o protect the Inform ation from unaut horised use, harm , expl oitation, m anipulation, m odification, interference, misuse, m isappropri ation, copy or di sclosure what soever, except as specifica lly au thorised by the disclosing part y in writing; (f) shall keep all In formation that is electronically stored in a tangible form segregat ed from all of its own propert y and i n a safe and secure envi ronm ent and protect and keep safe all such Inform ation from any loss, harm , theft, unaut horised use, t ampering, sabot age, unaut horised dupl ication, dest ruction, damage or interference whatsoever (g) shall, upon dem and, inform the disclosing part y of the location of t he Inform ation and t he measures that it h as taken to preserv e its co nfidentiality; (h) shall retu rn to the disclosing party all In formation, copi es and t angible manifestations t hereof, i n any form whatsoever, an d delete all In formation from all retriev al system s and databases o r destroy the same immediately upon t he direction of t he disclosing part y, and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be; (i) shall treat co mmunicatio ns with the other party as co nfidential an d utilise reaso nable care in maintaining the confidentiality o f communicatio ns even if th ey do not include Information: and (j) shall be liable to the other party fo r compliance with the term s and conditions of this Ag reem ent, and shall fully indem nify the other part y and hol d it harmless from dam ages (i ncluding lawyers’ fees and disbursem ents) incurred as a result of a breach of this Agreem ent.

perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase ', and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be;' from section 4(h), it removes the obligation for a senior officer or director to certify the destruction or return of confidential information, and by removing this clause, it becomes more difficult for the disclosing party to verify compliance and enforce the return or destruction of information. The remaining text obligates returning or destroying the information, but lacks verification. This is a direct contradiction within section 4, weakening enforcement capabilities.

(i) shall treat co mmunicatio ns with the other party as co nfidential an d utilise reaso nable care in maintaining the confidentiality o f communicatio ns even if th ey do not include Information: and (j) shall be liable to the other party fo r compliance with the term s and conditions of this Ag reem ent, and shall fully indem nify the other part y and hol d it harmless from dam ages (i ncluding lawyers’ fees and disbursem ents) incurred as a result of a breach of this Agreem ent.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section (h) included a requirement for a senior officer or director to certify the return, deletion, or destruction of confidential information. The changed text removes this certification requirement. This creates an in-text contradiction because, while the obligation to return or destroy the information remains, the removal of the certification process weakens the enforcement and verification of compliance, essentially creating a contradiction in how the original intent of confidentiality is maintained and verified. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language was removed that contradicts the obligations stated in other sections regarding maintaining strict confidentiality. The 'Contradicted Text' is the changed text itself.

perturbation.original_text: The parties’ obligations hereunder shall: (a) be unlimited in territo ry; (b) only expi re ten (10) y ears from the dat e of this Agreem ent or in the case of technical Inform ation at the end of t he com mercial useful ness t o the disclosing part y; and (c) survive the term ination of this Agreem ent for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties’ obligations hereunder shall: (a) be unlimited in territo ry; and (c) survive the term ination of this Agreem ent for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: By removing section 6 (b), which set the duration of obligations to 'ten (10) years from the date of this Agreement or in the case of technical information at the end of the commercial usefulness to the disclosing party,' this creates an ambiguity and thus a contradiction. The text now states that the obligations survive termination, but there is no fixed expiration date. This in-text contradiction makes the duration of the NDA unclear. It is indefinite now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties’ obligations hereunder shall: (a) be unlimited in territo ry; and (c) survive the term ination of this Agreem ent for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a duration of 'ten (10) years from the date of this Agreement or in the case of technical information at the end of the commercial usefulness to the disclosing party.' By removing this duration clause in the changed text, the agreement now lacks a defined expiration. The contradicted text states that the obligations survive termination, but without the original duration clause, there's no clear endpoint. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement implies perpetual obligation, conflicting with a reasonable expectation of a limited duration, leading to uncertainty about the NDA's enforceability. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties ag ree th at they sh all be entitled , in addition to all o ther rights provided by law o r by this Agreem ent, including m onetary dam ages, t o obtain a perm anent injunct ion or m andatory court order to prevent the violation of t he obl igations cont ained herei n.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties ag ree th at they sh all be entitled , in addition to all o ther rights provided by law o r by this Agreem ent, to obtain a perm anent injunct ion or m andatory court order to prevent the violation of t he obl igations cont ained herei n.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'including monetary damages' from Section 8, it creates uncertainty about whether the disclosing party can seek monetary compensation in addition to injunctive relief. While the remaining text allows for injunctive relief, the explicit removal of monetary damages implies that such damages may not be recoverable, even if other parts of the agreement mention indemnification (4j), thus potentially limiting the remedies available and creates an in-text contradiction. This conflicts with generally applicable law, by removing the possiblity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties ag ree th at they sh all be entitled , in addition to all o ther rights provided by law o r by this Agreem ent, to obtain a perm anent injunct ion or m andatory court order to prevent the violation of t he obl igations cont ained herei n.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly includes 'including monetary damages' as an option for the disclosing party. The changed text omits this phrase. While the changed text allows for injunctive relief and references 'other rights provided by law,' the explicit removal of 'including monetary damages' creates an ambiguity and a potential contradiction. The contradicted text is in the same location and the change creates a contradiction based on the given explanation.
